title: Sentinel Shield command-result envelope
description: Uniform, machine-readable result envelope emitted to STDOUT as ONE JSON object by any Sentinel Shield CLI command invoked with '--output json'. It is a RESULT (produced after the command runs), never a plan. It carries NO secrets and NO absolute local paths: warnings/artifacts/next_actions are redacted (HOME relativized to '~', the run's --target root to '<target>', and secret shapes masked). The process exit code is unchanged by '--output json'; this envelope only classifies it. exit_category is a stable coarse bucket; reason_codes are stable machine tokens (never free text).

command.description: The canonical command name that produced this envelope (e.g. 'doctor', 'run-local-pipeline').

version.description: Engine version the command reports (SENTINEL_SHIELD_VERSION, default '2.0.0').

status.description: Coarse outcome: ok (exit 0), warn (succeeded with non-blocking warnings), error (a blocking condition; exit non-zero).

exit_category.description: Stable classification of the process exit code, uniform across commands. success=clean; warnings=non-blocking; invalid_input=bad invocation/config; requirements_unmet=a required tool/dependency absent; execution_error=an operation/runner failed or was interrupted; not_ready=a promotion gate is unmet; findings=security findings blocked the gate.

reason_codes.description: Ordered stable machine tokens explaining the result (the primary reason first, then derived signals such as 'has_warnings'/'has_failures'). Never free text.

warnings.description: User-facing warnings surfaced by the run, redacted. May be empty.

artifacts.description: Paths of files the command reported writing, redacted/relativized. May be empty.

next_actions.description: Redacted next-step guidance surfaced by the run (e.g. doctor's 'Next:' line, override instructions). May be empty.

timestamp.description: Optional ISO-8601 UTC time the envelope was produced.
